name: Lemon Tartlets (Ew)
recipeInstructions:
1. To make lemon curd, whisk together egg, egg whites, sugar, lemon juice, and zest in a heavy-bottomed saucepan. If using a non-heavy-bottomed pan, use a double boiler and increase cooking time slightly. Add butter and cook over medium-low heat, whisking constantly until thickened (5-7 minutes). Let cool, cover, and refrigerate for up to 2 days.
2. To make pastry shells, place oven rack in the upper third of the oven and preheat to 375°F. Oil or spray 24 three-inch muffin cups.
3. In a medium saucepan, combine water, oil, sugar, and salt; bring to a boil over medium heat. Remove from heat and add flour all at once, stirring until a smooth mass forms. Let cool for 2 minutes.
4. Transfer mixture to a stand mixer with paddle attachment or food processor. Whisk eggs, egg whites, and vanilla in a separate bowl. Gradually add egg mixture to the dough, beating until incorporated. Repeat with additional portions until batter just falls from a spoon (lumpy).
5. Press about 1 tablespoon pastry into each muffin cup. Dip a wet spoon in cold water and press into the sides of the cups. With moistened fingers, press pastry part way up the sides to form 3/8-inch thick shells.
6. Bake for 5 minutes, then prick centers with a fork. Return to oven and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes or until puffed and golden. Loosen shells and transfer to a wire rack; cool.
7. Assemble tartlets by spooning about 1 tablespoon lemon curd into each shell and dusting with confectioners' sugar. Makes 24 tartlets, serving 12.
Entered by: Diane Pahl
